data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Regional Caution Weighs On Sentiment
data.description:
Singapore equities opened on a softer note on Monday, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) slipping 16.22 points or 0.33% to 4,973.19, as cautious sentiment lingered across global markets amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

Market breadth was negative, with decliners outpacing advancers 169 to 80, while total trading volume stood at 149.87 million shares valued at S$222.41 million in early trade.

Key index constituents were mixed, with DBS easing 0.296% to S$57.18, while other heavyweights such as Singtel, OCBC Bank and UOB saw limited movement in early deals.

The subdued opening comes against a backdrop of uncertain global cues, as investors remain wary over developments in the Middle East, particularly tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and its implications on oil supply and inflation.

Overnight, US index futures pointed to a largely flat open, reflecting a lack of clear direction as investors weighed geopolitical risks against recent gains in major indices. While Wall Street ended the previous session higher, sentiment remained fragile amid concerns over potential disruptions to global energy markets.

Across Asia, markets showed a mixed performance, with Hong Kong and China posting modest gains, while Australia and New Zealand ended slightly lower. Japan, however, outperformed on optimism over corporate earnings and continued investment in the technology sector.

In the derivatives market, trading activity remained moderate, with total volume at 29.64 thousand contracts. The FTSE China A50 Index Futures April 2026 contract stood at 14,976.00, reflecting cautious positioning among investors tracking regional equities.

data.title: Gold Loses Shine As Oil Rally Shakes Rate Outlook
data.description:
Gold prices retreated on Monday, pressured by a firmer US dollar and rising oil prices that have reignited inflation concerns, reducing expectations for interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year.

Spot gold fell 0.4% to US$4,726.64 per ounce, after earlier touching its lowest level since April 7 at US$4,643. US gold futures for June delivery declined 0.8% to US$4,748.70.

The decline comes as oil prices surged back above US$100 a barrel following the collapse of US-Iran peace talks, with the US preparing a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that could disrupt Iranian oil flows. The move has strengthened the dollar by 0.3%, making dollar-denominated bullion more expensive for other currency holders.

Iran’s Revolutionary Guards warned that any military vessels approaching the strait would be treated as a breach of the ceasefire and dealt with harshly and decisively, adding to market tensions.

“Ceasefire optimism has unwound following the failure of the peace talks, and the resulting push higher by the dollar and oil prices has put gold on the back foot again,” said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

He added that once oil prices move above US$100, attention shifts to potential central bank rate hikes to curb inflation, which weighs on gold’s performance.

Traders are now pricing in limited chances of rate cuts this year, a shift from earlier expectations of two reductions before the Middle East conflict escalated in late February. Higher energy prices are seen feeding into broader inflation, reducing room for monetary easing.

Since the conflict began, gold has declined more than 11%, as elevated interest rates continue to dampen demand for the non-yielding metal despite ongoing geopolitical risks.

Among other precious metals, silver dropped 1.9% to US$74.41 per ounce, platinum edged down 0.2% to US$2,041.89, while palladium rose 0.5% to US$1,527.95.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Today: Alliance Bank Slips 1.52% In Afternoon Trade
data.description:
Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd extended its weakness in afternoon trading on Monday, with the counter closing at RM4.54, down 1.52% or 7 sen, as investors booked profits amid broader caution in financial stocks.

The stock opened at RM4.60 and briefly matched that level as its intraday high before sliding to a low of RM4.52. It settled just above the day’s low, with the bid at RM4.54 and ask at RM4.55, reflecting a tight but slightly negative spread at close.

Trading volume came in at 126,190 lots, suggesting moderately active participation compared with recent sessions. The last done price of RM4.54 also marked a pullback from its recent short-term trading range, with the stock having previously shown resilience around the RM4.60 to RM5.00 band over the past months.

From a broader view, Alliance Bank’s share price has been oscillating within a mid-range channel over the past year, with periods of consolidation following earlier gains in the financial sector. The latest move places the counter below its recent local average cost level of RM4.61, indicating mild near-term selling pressure.

Market sentiment across banking stocks has been mixed, with investors weighing interest rate expectations, margin stability and loan growth outlooks. The latest session reflected cautious positioning rather than sector-wide weakness, as liquidity remained steady but skewed towards selling.

At current levels, the stock remains within its broader multi-month range, with traders likely watching for sustained support around the RM4.50 region and a clearer directional catalyst from upcoming banking sector earnings or macroeconomic signals.

data.title: Wall Street Futures Drop After US-Iran Talks Fail
data.description:
Wall Street futures slipped on Monday, starting the week on a subdued note, after talks between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend failed to deliver a deal to end the war.

The declines indicate that any relief from the ceasefire reached last week could be fleeting, and underscore the risks of leaning too heavily into bullish bets when the geopolitical environment remains uncertain.The U.S. military is hours away from beginning a blockade of all maritime traffic entering or leaving Iranian ports and coastal areas, in a move aimed at ramping up pressure on Tehran.

“For the Iranians who up to now have felt that time is on their side, it puts pressure on their allies to encourage Iran to come to the table to make a deal,” said William Blair’s macro analyst Richard de Chazal.

As of 04:25 a.m. ET, Dow E-minis were down 200 points, or 0.42 per cent. Nasdaq 100 E-minis declined 163 points, or 0.64 per cent, while S&P 500 E-minis fell 0.53 per cent, or 36 points.

The shift in sentiment was also visible across other asset classes, with investors gravitating toward nL6N40W0BU the safe-haven U.S. dollar while trimming exposure to equities across geographies nL4N40W0EA.

Oil prices jumped back above nL1N40V07F $100 a barrel, aggravating inflation worries after data last week showed that a record surge in the cost of gasoline and diesel prompted the biggest increase in consumer prices in nearly four years in March.Investors are also focused on the start of the U.S. earnings season, with Goldman Sachs scheduled to report before the bell. Commentary from the investment banking giant’s executives will be parsed for clues on how the Middle East conflict, now in its seventh week, is impacting the economy and the capital markets.

Goldman shares were marginally higher in premarket trading but outperformed most of its Wall Street peers.

Travel-related stocks fell, with carriers such as Delta Air Lines and JetBlue Airways down 2.2 per cent and 3.8 per cent, respectively, on concerns that higher oil prices might push up fuel costs.

Energy stocks gained, with Chevron, Exxon Mobil and ConocoPhillips climbing 2.3 per cent, 2.6 per cent and 2.8 per cent, respectively.

Reuters

data.title: Ringgit Opens Mostly Higher Against Major Currencies, Lower Against Dollar
data.description:
The ringgit opened mostly higher against major currencies today, but eased slightly against the US dollar amid growing external uncertainties and firmer demand for the US dollar, Bernama reported.

At 8 am, the local note eased to 3.9695/9960 against the US dollar from 3.9625/9680 at last Friday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit’s near-term performance is likely to remain cautious amid heightened geopolitical tensions and shifting market sentiment.

“Risk aversion among traders is becoming more pronounced following stalled negotiations between the US and Iran. Historically, such developments tend to trigger safe-haven demand for the greenback,” he told Bernama.

He said the shift in sentiment follows escalating geopolitical risks, including reports that US President Donald Trump is considering a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a move aimed at pressuring Iran’s economy via the oil and gas trade, which has pushed both WTI and Brent crude benchmarks above US$100 per barrel this morning.

Mohd Afzanizam said the development has also strengthened the US dollar, with the US Dollar Index (DXY) rising 0.47 per cent to 99.115 points.

“Therefore, it is going to be a risk-off mode today, and the ringgit could see depreciation against the greenback and perhaps revisit the RM4.00 level,” he added.

It was reported that the US Central Command would begin implementing a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports on April 13 at 10 am (Eastern US Time), to be enforced impartially against vessels of all nations across Iranian ports and coastal areas, including those along the Arabian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman.

At the opening, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

It rose against the British pound to 5.3167/3522 compared with 5.3248/3322, improved against the Japanese yen to 2.4858/5025 from 2.4882/4918, and strengthened against the euro to 4.6344/6653 from 4.6401/6465, from last Friday’s close.

However, the local currency opened mixed against ASEAN currencies.

It appreciated versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1065/1275 from 3.1093/1139 and rose to 12.2587/3493 from 12.3289/3525 against the Thai baht. However, against the Indonesian rupiah, it fell to 232.0/233.7 from 231.6/232.0, and was lower against the Philippine peso at 6.61/6.66 from 6.60/6.62 previously.

data.title: Bursa Slips As Middle East Tensions Rattle Markets
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on April 13, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) falling 10.79 points, or 0.64% to 1,680.52, as escalating geopolitical tensions weighed on regional sentiment.

The decline, tracking weaker Asian markets, came after Iran-US talks ended in a stalemate, prompting Washington to move ahead with plans to blockade the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over global oil supply and market stability.

Broader indices also closed in the red, with the FBM 70 down 124.12 points (0.72%) to 17,196.85, FBM EMAS slipping 79.39 points (0.64%) to 12,333.27 and FBM Shariah easing 22.27 points (0.18%) to 12,267.06, reflecting widespread selling pressure across sectors.

Market activity, however, remained robust, with turnover rising to 2.94 billion shares worth RM2.64 billion compared to 2.89 billion shares valued at RM2.6 billion in the previous session, suggesting active repositioning amid heightened uncertainty.

Trading was led by speculative and retail-driven names, with MMAG Holdings Bhd topping the most active list, followed by Zetrix AI Bhd.

Glove maker Top Glove Corp Bhd saw heavy trading but ended lower, while Bumi Armada Bhd advanced amid active trading. Property-linked Tanco Holdings Bhd also featured among the most traded counters but closed slightly weaker.

The market’s decline underscores investor caution as geopolitical risks intensify, particularly with potential disruptions to a key global oil transit route.

With external uncertainties dominating sentiment, Bursa is likely to remain volatile in the near term as investors monitor developments in the Middle East and broader regional market cues.

data.title: Tesla Stock Drops For Eighth Week In A Row
data.description:
Tesla stock dropped for the eighth week in a row as its rough 2026 start rolls on and first quarter earnings come in later this month.

Tesla stock, though slightly higher in early Friday trade, is down 3.2% for the week, notching its eighth straight week of losses. The stock is down a 22.4% for the year, whereas the S&P 500 is basically flat.

While Tesla does trade seasonally in most years, with the first half usually challenged, bulls will be hard on the lookout for catalysts, seeking anything to get the stock movingThe Elon Musk-led company is slated to reveal version three of its Optimus robot sometime this month. No updates on that have been seen recently, leaving an April update in limbo. Meanwhile, Tesla has not sold a single robot yet, though Musk claims robots are being used in limited capacities in Tesla factories.

Tesla’s Cybercab did hit its production milestone in Q1, with reports showing Cybercabs sitting in parking lots at Giga Austin. When these EVs will be on sale is another story, as robotaxi updates have also been hard to come by.

Tesla is still operating its robotaxi and ride-hailing services in Austin, Texas, and the San Francisco Bay Area, but the rides are still supervised with safety drivers in each vehicle

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Crypto Rally Cools As Bitcoin, Ethereum Loses Momentum
data.description:
The trading session for crypto market on April 13 saw a sharp shift in sentiment, with Bitcoin and Ethereum both retreating after last week’s geopolitical-driven rally, highlighting the market’s continued sensitivity to macro signals and trader positioning.

Bitcoin fell to around US$70,600, down roughly 3%, slipping back under the key US$71,000 level after briefly reclaiming it days earlier. The move marks a cooling-off from a ceasefire-fuelled surge that had pushed the token above US$71K-US$72K range, as traders locked in profits and risk appetite softened.

Ethereum, meanwhile, tracked the broader pullback, hovering near US$2,200 and posting a slight daily decline of about 0.7%. The second-largest cryptocurrency faced additional pressure from derivatives markets, where open interest dropped nearly 7% in 24 hours, signalling traders were cutting exposure amid rising caution.

The downturn comes just days after a strong rally triggered by easing geopolitical tensions, which had lifted Bitcoin by about 4% and Ethereum by over 6%. But that optimism appears to be fading, with markets now grappling with mixed signals ranging from macro uncertainty to fragile investor sentiment.

Analysts note that Bitcoin remains above a crucial support zone near US$69,000, a level seen as pivotal for maintaining bullish momentum. Meanwhile, Ethereum’s weakening derivatives activity suggests institutional and leveraged players are turning defensive, at least in the short term.

Bottom line: The trading session on April 13 underscores a familiar crypto pattern — rapid gains followed by equally swift pullbacks. With sentiment swinging between geopolitical relief and macro caution, both Bitcoin and Ethereum remain in a volatile consolidation phase, leaving traders bracing for the next decisive catalyst.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 13, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 13, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As Losers Swamp Gainers 760 To 402
data.description:
Selling pressure gripped Bursa Malaysia, with decliners decisively outpacing gainers 760 to 402, signalling a cautious market as investors pared positions across key sectors.

Market breadth remained weak, with 454 counters unchanged, 1,101 untraded and 12 suspended, reflecting the lack of strong buying momentum.

Losses were spearheaded by Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, which saw the steepest decline among top losers at 62 sen to close the trading session at RM29.18, followed by plantation-linked Batu Kawan Bhd and banking heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd.

Financial and consumer names also came under pressure, with Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd and Apollo Food Holdings Bhd rounding out the top decliners, pointing to broad-based weakness.

On the upside, selective buying emerged in defensive and energy-related counters. Nestle (M) Bhd led gainers, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, PMB Technology Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and Petronas Gas Bhd.

The market’s negative breadth highlights a defensive stance among investors, with gains concentrated in a handful of blue chips while broader selling persists.

Overall, sentiment remains fragile, with the imbalance between losers and gainers suggesting continued consolidation in the near term.

data.title: FBM KLCI Slips 13.35 Points At Midday As Broad-Based Selling Drags Market Lower
data.description:
Malaysian equities traded weaker at midday, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) sliding 13.35 points, or 0.79%, to 1,677.96, as selling pressure weighed across most index components and broader market segments.

The benchmark index retreated from its close of 1,691.31 on April 10, tracking a softer regional tone and persistent profit-taking across heavyweight and mid-cap counters.

Broader market indices also mirrored the decline, with the FBM 70 down 154.12 points (0.89%) to 17,166.85, FBM EMAS slipping 100.29 points (0.81%) to 12,312.37, and FBM Shariah easing 57.69 points (0.47%) to 12,231.64, signalling widespread weakness across the board.

Market breadth also turned decisively negative, with 751 losers trouncing 299 gainers, while 390 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,277 stocks were untraded, and 12 were suspended, underscoring cautious sentiment among investors.

Trading activity remained active, with 1.72 billion shares changing hands, valued at RM1.21 billion.

Meanwhile, retail-driven and speculative counters dominated trading volume. MMAG Holdings Bhd led the pack with heavy turnover despite a marginal gain, while Bumi Armada Bhd also attracted strong interest, edging higher in active trade.

However, several high-volume names such as VS Industry Bhd and Top Glove Corporation Bhd slipped, reflecting mixed sentiment in export and manufacturing-related counters.

On the gainers’ board, defensive heavyweights provided some cushion. Nestle (M) Bhd led advances, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd, which saw selective buying interest.

Meanwhile, losses in Hong Leong Bank Bhd, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd and Sunway Construction Group Bhd weighed on sentiment, highlighting weakness in financial and construction-linked stocks.

Overall, the market’s midday tone points to consolidation after recent gains, with investors rotating into defensive plays while trimming exposure in cyclical and mid-cap names.

data.title: Market Review: Hang Seng In Consolidation Mode As Trade Tensions Persist
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks closed the April 6-10 trading week on a softer note, with the Hang Seng Index (HSI) edging lower amid persistent US-China trade tensions and cautious investor sentiment.

The market traded unevenly throughout the week, with early gains giving way to profit-taking as tariff fears and Wall Street volatility dampened risk appetite.

Tech and property stocks led declines, reflecting sensitivity to global growth concerns, while financials and select defensives provided limited support.

Turnover remained moderate as investors adopted a defensive stance, closely tracking external cues from Wall Street and mainland economic data.

Overall, the HSI stayed in consolidation mode, with sentiment fragile as markets await a clearer direction on trade policies and their broader economic impact.

data.title: Stock Today: PChem Powers Higher Past RM6 As EPF Rebalances Stakes
data.description:
Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PCHEM) traded higher in the morning session, rising 3.26% or 19 sen to RM6.01 as of the time of writing, amid active turnover and steady buying interest in early trade.

The counter opened at RM6.02, briefly climbed to an intraday high of RM6.13 and touched a low of RM6.00 before stabilising near current levels, suggesting firm support around the RM6.00 mark. Trading volume reached 135,418 lots, reflecting heightened participation compared with recent sessions, while bid and offer levels were tightly positioned at RM6.00 and RM6.01 respectively, indicating balanced order flow at current price ranges.

The Last Closing Price (LACP) stood at RM5.82, highlighting the strength of the current rebound in the session. Market activity showed sustained interest throughout the morning with the stock holding most of its gains after the initial surge, supported by steady accumulation at the lower end of the trading range.

Separately, filing data showed the Employees Provident Fund Board (EPF Board) adjusted its shareholding in PCHEM through a mix of transactions on 7 April 2026. The fund disposed of 342,300 shares via Citigroup Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd (Nomura), while acquiring 1,000,000 shares through CIMB PRI and 2,504,800 shares via Aberdeen.

Despite the internal reshuffling across nominee accounts, EPF Board’s total direct interest remained unchanged at 1,014,691,908 shares, equivalent to 12.684% of the company’s issued capital.

data.title: Weekly Snapshot: STI Trades Range-Bound As Caution Persists Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description:
Singapore stocks ended the week of April 6–10 on a steady but cautious note, with the Straits Times Index (STI) moving within a narrow range as investors weighed mixed global cues and interest rate uncertainties.

The market saw choppy, range-bound trading throughout the week, with early stability capped by profit-taking toward the later sessions as sentiment remained guarded. Concerns over global trade developments and macroeconomic signals kept gains modest.

Banking heavyweights, including DBS Group Holdings, Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation and United Overseas Bank, provided underlying support, helping to anchor the index, while technology and consumer counters faced intermittent selling pressure. Commodity-linked stocks, meanwhile, saw selective interest.

Trading activity remained moderate, reflecting a wait-and-see approach as investors assessed global inflation signals and the policy outlook from the Federal Reserve. External factors, including Wall Street performance and regional market trends, continued to guide direction.

Despite pockets of resilience, the overall tone remained cautious and selective, with investors remaining defensive while awaiting clearer signals on global growth and the trajectory of monetary policy.

data.title: Stock Today: AirAsia X Sinks 4.13%
data.description:
AirAsia X Bhd came under sustained selling pressure in Monday trading, at RM1.16, down 4.13% or 5 sen as investors locked in profits amid elevated volume.

The stock opened at RM1.16, briefly touched an intraday high of RM1.18 before sliding to a low of RM1.15 where it found some support into the close. The counter ended just above its session low, reflecting persistent downside pressure throughout the day.

Trading activity was notably strong, with volume reaching 3.05 million lots, signalling heightened participation from both retail and institutional investors. Despite the heavy turnover, selling momentum dominated as the bid-ask spread remained tight at RM1.15 to RM1.16, indicating continued interest at lower levels but limited upward buying strength.

At current levels, AirAsia X has retreated from its recent higher trading range, with the latest close marking a clear step down from previous resistance zones around RM1.20 to RM1.30. The pullback places the stock closer to its short-term support band, suggesting the market is testing investor appetite at lower valuations.

The broader chart trend over the reviewed period shows volatility with sharp swings rather than a sustained directional move, reflecting shifting sentiment in the airline and travel-linked segment as investors react to cost pressures, demand expectations and sector recovery timing.

data.title: Market Recap: Bursa Edges Higher Amid Trade Jitters And Choppy Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia wrapped up the week of April 6 to 10 on a steady note, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) posting modest gains as investors weighed global trade jitters and volatile external cues.

The benchmark index saw choppy movements throughout the week, reflecting mixed sentiment driven by US tariff fears and firmer commodity prices. Early-week advances were tempered by profit-taking and selective positioning toward the end of the week.

Trading activity remained moderate, with investors adopting a wait-and-see approach amid uncertainty over inflation signals and global growth outlook.

Sector-wise, plantation counters drew interest on the back of elevated crude palm oil prices, while consumer and technology stocks faced intermittent selling pressure. Banking heavyweights provided some support, helping to limit broader downside.

Market direction continued to be influenced by external factors, particularly Wall Street performance and commodity market volatility, with Bursa often mirroring overnight global trends.

Overall, the week underscored a resilient tone in the market, as investors remained selective and defensive, awaiting clearer signals on the trajectory of global trade tensions and their impact on Malaysia’s economic outlook.

data.title: Seoul Stocks Rally 1.4% As Ceasefire Optimism Lifts Sentiment
data.description:
South Korean equities surged on April 10, tracking Wall Street gains as optimism over potential US-Iran negotiations buoyed investor sentiment following a fragile Middle East ceasefire.

The Kospi jumped 1.4% to 5,858.87, extending its weekly rally to nearly 9%, as risk appetite returned despite lingering geopolitical uncertainty.

Foreign investors led the charge, snapping up 1.1 trillion won in shares, while institutions and retail investors were net sellers. Market breadth was firmly positive, with gainers outpacing losers more than four-to-one.

Tech heavyweights drove the advance, with Samsung Electronics rising 0.98% and SK hynix climbing 2.91%, while gains in defence and shipping stocks further supported the benchmark.

Investor confidence was underpinned by signs of easing tensions in the Middle East, including moves toward dialogue involving Israel and Lebanon, reinforcing hopes that the ceasefire could hold.

The Korean won held steady at 1,482.5 per dollar, while bond yields edged higher, reflecting a mild shift toward risk-on sentiment.

Bottom line: Seoul stocks extended their rally on improving geopolitical sentiment, with foreign inflows and tech gains anchoring the market’s strong weekly performance.

data.title: Dollar Jumps As Failed US/Iran Peace Talks Spark Fresh Safe-Haven Push
data.description:
The dollar jumped against other major currencies in thin trading late on Sunday, as investors sought the relative safety of the U.S. currency after marathon talks between ​Washington and Tehran failed to yield a peace deal, plunging markets into a seventh ‌week of uncertainty.

President Donald Trump on Sunday said the U.S. Navy would start blockading the Strait of Hormuz, a choke point for 20% of the world’s daily energy supplies that Iran effectively closed since the war started in ​late February. That has driven oil prices up by over 30% and fuelled fears of ​a widespread surge in inflation.

The dollar, which has acted as a safe haven ⁠given the limited exposure of the United States to imported energy-price inflation, rallied as Asian markets ​opened for trading, leaving the euro down 0.53% at $1.1663 and gaining 0.1% against the Japanese yen to ​trade at 159.43.

The U.S. and Iran on April 7 announced a two-week ceasefire that investors initially cheered by selling oil and putting some capital back into risk assets such as stocks. Concern over the fragility of the deal ​has since prompted an unwinding of some of those trades.

“This is an absolute unwinding of any ​optimism heading into the peace talks into that play of dollar: safe-haven; oil jumping and selling out of everything ‌else,” ⁠City Index senior market analyst Fiona Cincotta said.

“On the other hand, we have seen the markets over-exaggerate sometimes. And I think especially around this scenario, the market is struggling to really price it correctly, because there is so much uncertainty, so many unknowns.”

More risk-sensitive currencies such as the Australian dollar ​and sterling came under heavy ​pressure, falling 1.1% ⁠and 0.5%, respectively.

With expectations building for a resurgence in inflation, investors have priced in the possibility of several central banks, such as the European Central ​Bank and Bank of England, leaning towards raising interest rates this year, ​in stark ⁠contrast with expectations prior to the war for borrowing rates to remain unchanged or fall.

Global equities which ended last week around their highest since early March, buoyed by optimism that the United States and ⁠Iran were ​heading towards some kind of resolution, are still 2% below where they ​were prior to the war breaking out.

Gold has lost about 10% in value since late February, as investors see the ​dollar as a better safe-haven right now.

Reuters

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Close Higher As Gains Return
data.description:
Hong Kong equities rebounded on April 10, snapping a cautious spell as investor sentiment improved modestly despite lingering global uncertainties.

The Hang Seng Index rose 0.55% to 25,893.54, gaining 141.14 points, with buying interest returning to select large-cap names and lifting the broader market.

The advance comes even as investors remain wary of external headwinds, including geopolitical tensions and energy-driven inflation risks. Still, the day’s gains suggest pockets of resilience, supported by bargain hunting and selective inflows.

Bottom line: Hong Kong stocks edged higher, but the recovery remains tentative as global risks continue to cap stronger upside momentum.

data.title: Bursa Opens Lower As Geopolitical Jitters Weigh On Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower on Monday, tracking weaker regional sentiment as investors reacted to ongoing geopolitical tensions and cautious global risk appetite.

At 9.18 am, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) slipped 6.29 points or 0.37% to 1,685.02.

Broader market indices also moved lower across the board. The FBM70 fell 108.94 points or 0.63% to 17,212.03, while the FBMEMAS Index eased 52.49 points or 0.42% to 12,360.17. The FBM Shariah Index declined 32.97 points or 0.27% to 12,256.36, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index dropped 4.28 points or 0.43% to 997.59.

Market breadth was negative, with decliners dominating gainers, reflecting cautious investor positioning at the start of trade.

Trading activity was relatively active in early deals, with MMAG among the most heavily traded counters, unchanged at 4.5 sen despite active volume. SUNMED slipped 1 sen to RM1.89, while TOPGLOV eased 0.5 sen to 80 sen. HANDAL was flat at 3.5 sen, and OGM rose 1 sen to 15 sen.

Among index movers, UTDPLT led gainers, up 34 sen to RM33.34, followed by PETDAG which added 26 sen to RM21.06 and KLK which gained 20 sen to RM22.14. PCHEM advanced 20 sen to RM6.02 while SDG rose 13 sen to RM6.12.

On the losing end, MPI slipped 40 sen to RM29.40, while NESTLE fell 30 sen to RM97.80. HLBANK dropped 22 sen to RM21.86, and F&N and HEIM each eased 20 sen to RM29.60 and RM22.96 respectively.

Sentiment in the wider region remained cautious as markets continued to digest developments surrounding US-Iran negotiations, which ended without a deal, alongside heightened concerns over oil supply risks after reports of a potential US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
